explanations.11: Optional[oracledb.Connection] means this variable starts as None but will hold a database connection object once we connect.
explanations.21: AuthMode.SYSDBA is a special permission level that gives us admin access to the Oracle database.
explanations.29: ROWNUM <= :max_rows is Oracle's way of limiting results; the colon means we'll pass the actual number separately as a parameter.
explanations.35: We're telling Python that fetchall() will return a list of tuples, where each tuple has four values: an int and three strings.
explanations.46: Unpacking takes one tuple from the list and splits it into four separate variables in a single assignment.
explanations.47: The <12, <20, <26 format specifiers left-align text in fields of that width, creating neat columns.
